feature: Home page should support guest state controls, guest navigation, and login entry from the Person button

userStory: Home page should support guest state controls, guest navigation, and login entry from the Person button

As a guest user, I want the Home page to display the guest-state controls and guest-accessible feature buttons, so that I can use the application before login and open the Login page from the Person/Profile button.
This application is a single page application (SPA).
The workflow begins at: http://localhost/washtabui/home The Home page is the primary landing page for both unauthenticated users and authenticated users.
This workflow covers only the guest-state behavior of the Home page before login.
The Home page in this workflow should be treated as the guest state of the existing Home page, not as a separate page from the authenticated Home state.
GUEST HOME STATE When the application is launched and the user has not signed in, the Home page should load in guest state.

In guest state, the Home page displays the approved guest and shared controls, including: Person/Profile button Back button Notification button Home button Guest-accessible feature buttons Known guest-accessible feature buttons include: Customer signup button Clean survey button Site survey button GUEST-STATE EXPECTATIONS In guest state: The Person/Profile button should be visible and enabled.
Clicking the Person/Profile button should open the Login page or Login view.
Guest-accessible feature buttons should be visible and enabled.
Guest-accessible feature buttons should navigate to their supported destinations.
Authenticated-only controls and authenticated-only feature buttons should not be visible or should not be actionable.

keywords: Verify Guest Home Controls Are Visible And Enabled
Verify Home Guest State Controls Are Visible
Verify Home Guest State Controls Are Enabled
Verify Guest Feature Buttons Are Ready
Verify Home Page Remains In Guest State

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home page loads successfully in guest state from the SPA landing URL
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home page loads successfully in guest state and displays the Person/Profile button, Back button, Notification button, Home button, Customer signup button, Clean survey button, and Site survey button as visible interactive controls.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify all approved guest-state controls are visible and enabled on Home page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Person/Profile button, Back button, Notification button, Home button, Customer signup button, Clean survey button, and Site survey button are visible, enabled, focusable, and appear actionable in guest state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Person/Profile button opens the Login page from guest Home state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application opens the Login page or Login view from the guest-state Home page without requiring prior authentication.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Customer signup button navigates to the customer-search page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application navigates from the guest-state Home page to the customer-search page.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Clean survey button navigates to the vehicle-survey page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application navigates from the guest-state Home page to the vehicle-survey page.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Site survey button navigates to the survey page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application navigates from the guest-state Home page to the survey page.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home page remains the same SPA Home resource in guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application continues to display the same Home page resource in guest state without redirecting to a separate authenticated Home implementation.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home button is visible and enabled in guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home button is visible and enabled, and clicking it keeps the user within the Home experience without breaking guest-state navigation.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Notification button is visible and enabled in guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Notification button is visible and enabled for interaction in guest state without causing application instability.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Back button is visible and enabled in guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Back button is visible and enabled in the shared Home shell while the application remains operational in guest state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify guest-accessible feature buttons remain actionable before authentication
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Each approved guest-accessible feature button remains enabled and successfully navigates to its supported destination while the user is unauthenticated.

validations: The Home page loads successfully in guest state and displays the Person/Profile button, Back button, Notification button, Home button, Customer signup button, Clean survey button, and Site survey button as visible interactive controls.

The Person/Profile button, Back button, Notification button, Home button, Customer signup button, Clean survey button, and Site survey button are visible, enabled, focusable, and appear actionable in guest state.
The Home button is visible and enabled, and clicking it keeps the user within the Home experience without breaking guest-state navigation.
The Notification button is visible and enabled for interaction in guest state without causing application instability.
The Back button is visible and enabled in the shared Home shell while the application remains operational in guest state.
Each approved guest-accessible feature button remains enabled and successfully navigates to its supported destination while the user is unauthenticated.
transitions: The Home page loads successfully in guest state and displays the Person/Profile button, Back button, Notification button, Home button, Customer signup button, Clean survey button, and Site survey button as visible interactive controls.

The Person/Profile button, Back button, Notification button, Home button, Customer signup button, Clean survey button, and Site survey button are visible, enabled, focusable, and appear actionable in guest state.
The application opens the Login page or Login view from the guest-state Home page without requiring prior authentication.
The application navigates from the guest-state Home page to the customer-search page.
The application navigates from the guest-state Home page to the vehicle-survey page.
The application navigates from the guest-state Home page to the survey page.
The application continues to display the same Home page resource in guest state without redirecting to a separate authenticated Home implementation.
The Notification button is visible and enabled for interaction in guest state without causing application instability.
The Back button is visible and enabled in the shared Home shell while the application remains operational in guest state.
Each approved guest-accessible feature button remains enabled and successfully navigates to its supported destination while the user is unauthenticated.

wrapperExpectations: Reuse approved shared/common wrapper keywords before low-level SeleniumLibrary actions.
Do not invent suite-level custom keywords; compose from approved upstream resource knowledge.
